title: In Peace and Joy I Now Depart

metadata.composer: 'Mit Fried und Freud ich Far Dahin' from Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein, 1524 Michael Praetorius, 1610
metadata.lyricist: Martin Luther, 1524.  Translated by Leonard Woolsey Bacon, 1884, alt
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2009 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Martin Luther, 1524.  Translated by Leonard Woolsey Bacon, 1884, alt.
Music: 'Mit Fried und Freud ich Far Dahin' from Walter's Geistliche Gesangbüchlein, 1524.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2009 Revision.

1.raw_text: In peace and joy I now depart At God's disposing; For full of comfort is my heart, Soft reposing. So the Lord hath promised me, And death is but a slumber.

2.raw_text: 'Tis Christ that wrought this work for me, My faithful Savior, Whom Thou hast made mine eyes to see By Thy favor. Now I know He is my Life, My Help in need and dying.

3.raw_text: Him Thou hast unto all set forth Their great Salvation And to His kingdom called the earth, Every nation, By Thy dear and wholesome Word, In every place resounding.

4.raw_text: He is the Hope and saving Light Of lands benighted; By Him are they who dwelt in night Fed and lighted. He is Israel's Praise and Bliss, Their Joy, Reward, and Glory.
